steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The claim states that Mrs. Sommers makes decisions “without conscious deliberation”—meaning she acts on automatic impulse rather than through deliberate reflection. The correct quotation must show her acting without thinking.

steps.title: Find the quotation that demonstrates this
steps.content: Choice C directly states: “She was not thinking at all” and describes her surrendering to “some mechanical impulse that directed her actions.” This quotation explicitly illustrates decision-making without conscious deliberation. The other three show her reacting to what she observes or examining details carefully, but none convey acting without thought. This matches choice C — C is correct.
